metadata.name: Dimir Guildgate enters the battlefield tapped (ETB self-replacement)
metadata.description: p1 plays Dimir Guildgate from hand. The land's self-ETB replacement (AbilityDefinition::Replacement with WouldEnterBattlefield+is_self:true) fires during the ETB path, causing the land to enter tapped. The permanent was never untapped — CR 614.1c: this is a replacement, not a trigger. Asserts Dimir Guildgate is on the battlefield and is tapped.
metadata.generation_notes: Tests the ETB self-replacement path (apply_self_etb_from_definition in lands.rs). Dimir Guildgate has AbilityDefinition::Replacement{trigger:WouldEnterBattlefield,modification:EntersTapped,is_self:true}. This is applied inline — the permanent was never in an untapped state on the battlefield. Corner case 19: 'enters tapped' is a replacement, not a trigger.

script.step_note: p1 plays Dimir Guildgate; ETB self-replacement causes it to enter tapped

actions.description: Initial: Dimir Guildgate in p1 hand

actions.note: CR 305.1: p1 plays Dimir Guildgate as a special action. ETB self-replacement fires: enters tapped.

actions.description: After playing: Dimir Guildgate on battlefield TAPPED (CR 614.1c: enters tapped, never untapped)
actions.note: CR 614.1c: 'enters the battlefield tapped' is a replacement effect, not a trigger. The land was NEVER untapped on the battlefield — it arrived already tapped.
